sources.notes: Delete the minimum number of elements so the remaining array has no i<j with b_j-b_i=1.
Luogu solution sorts by value/index and optimizes the DP transition with a Fenwick tree for prefix maxima.
Official editorial comments discuss the same good-subsequence DP structure over value groups.

annotation.summary: Remove as few elements as possible so the kept subsequence contains no earlier value x followed later by x+1.
annotation.constraints: Total n is 3e5, so the longest good subsequence must be computed in O(n log n).
annotation.core_idea: Keep the longest good subsequence and answer n minus its length. Sort elements by value ascending and equal values by original index descending. For current value v at original position p, it may extend any previous kept subsequence ending at value not equal to v-1, or one ending at v-1 whose position is after p. A global best over values at most v-2 and a Fenwick maximum over reversed positions for value v-1 optimize this transition.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per test case.
tricks: sort equal values by reverse index
split DP transition into far-value and previous-value cases

solution_variants.name: sorted-value-fenwick-dp
solution_variants.summary: Process elements by value, maintain best DP for values <=v-2 and Fenwick maxima for compatible positions of value v-1.

tags.evidence: The main task is a longest subsequence DP with value/order compatibility constraints.
Fenwick tree stores prefix maxima over original positions to optimize transitions from value v-1.
Sorting by value and reverse original index is required to make the DP order valid.
